name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: We won an important victory today
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

We won an important victory today for Texas and the rule of law.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251118.B_r73E&refcode=ema20251118-240470&t=1002&akid=240470%2E444211%2E9SxaSa ]NBC headline: Federal Court blocks Texas Republicans' redrawn
congressional map.

And now we are going to press the attack, continuing to fight on every
front and organize in every community with the intensity and urgency that
we’ll need to win the House of Representatives and ensure we finally have
a check on Trump’s lawlessness, accountability for his crimes and
corruption, and real hope for a better future for this country.

Winning in 2026 makes it possible to win in 2028, and winning in 2028
means we build a real democracy for this country—no more gerrymandering,
no more trying to stack the deck and rig the elections. It means an
economic democracy too—one where Texans can afford to work just one job,
afford to buy a home, afford to go to the doctor, and afford child care
too. If we stay together, stay on offense, there is no stopping the people
of Texas.

Powered by People is doing the work to make it happen. We’re registering
voters, recruiting candidates, signing up volunteers, and helping our
fellow Texans sift through the confusion. [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251118.B_r73E&refcode=ema20251118-240470&t=1003&akid=240470%2E444211%2E9SxaSa ]Will you pitch in

name: Tim Kaine
subject: The Senate just passed legislation to release the Epstein files
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
Derek, the Senate just passed the Epstein Files Transparency Act by unanimous consent.
The hundreds of girls and women who were abused and silenced by Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell deserve this — transparency, truth, and accountability.
I’m relieved members of both parties came together to pass this legislation swiftly.
After Trump’s longstanding efforts to keep these files under wraps and the DOJ and FBI’s refusal to comply with subpoenas, the President abruptly changed course and encouraged Republicans to vote for the release.
This is an incredibly important reminder that Donald Trump can be pushed to act — that public outcry still matters, and that sustained pressure can force real change.
Today, I’m relieved that Epstein’s survivors and victims will finally get the answers they are owed, and that the American public will see the accountability so many have righteously demanded.
Tomorrow, as we keep working to counter this Administration’s reckless maneuvers — like my ongoing push to block unauthorized war in Venezuela and undo Trump’s cost-raising tariffs — we must continue to turn up the pressure.
Thank you for your dedication to standing up and speaking out with me.
—Tim Kaine
Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
1490-5A Quarterpath Road #272
Williamsburg, VA 23185

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. I’ve never backed down from a fight — and I sure as hell won’t start now. But Stefanik will be watching us closely, and we need to usher in a record-breaking fundraising day to show Stefanik she doesn’t stand a chance. **[That’s why I’m calling on my top supporters to help me reach my goal of 1,000 gifts before midnight and prove the strength of our team. Can I count on you to split a $10 donation between me and Fight Like Hell PAC? >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119533367/583379481/-1344123639?refcode=AU_FLHP_FR_TAN_GEN_NO_20251118_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY0NjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWQzMzk3NDItZDNjNC1mMDExLTgxOTYtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=piJOdL9y_bRMNSnZ4pI9qrBSjkUTMEbjsX3QLt7T91A=)** I’ll be blunt: Sellout Stefanik is a self-serving politician who cares more about Donald Trump’s approval than what’s best for New York families. She supports Trump’s expensive tariffs that are jacking up the costs of everything from clothing to groceries for New Yorkers. She voted multiple times against the Child Tax Credit. Stefanik brags about being the “deciding vote” for his agenda that will rip away health care from 1.5 million New Yorkers and put 5 rural hospitals in her district on the chopping block. And when the Trump administration threatened to cut NY police funding, Stefanik stayed silent. **New Yorkers need a governor who will stand up to Trump and fight like hell for them.** **[I need all hands on deck to fight back. name: Maxwell Frost
subject: sorry I don’t own any stocks?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MAXWELL FROST FOR CONGRESS
body:
| [ ](http://clicksp.frostforcongress.com/f/a/q056wyaL4szgTP7FJSubpg~~/AAPPABA~/e_Qwigg1PW1GKTZH8fGAnUKG-fgto0cyZnCVGw1agVVOB9A9t7pspdAs1OfJi8VUC0KFVKlEL5Q2Up1pAfWcD78yBaz_nxaEMqybqcYpWVHfedQSFezhPKcshc4ldUuS4IiA7EWwCy4X8Y_wLVIJCNMJ2_p92xR2A8ztkyCgsGijLI8Z0qcKjw3aoPrGrnwLf4Wl4T_SRanWBwbBstVwNqV42sKOf2vxfarmq5qoR01-F9YsB6hQW-qkgOCQjefSBwadGn2VQRz-JQwfzBbEp4wOzK3dOyyuUIc2syy5J-klrDOL3WpuujrU_FjSXFLQcCzelIHCwD7i1v6SipZdFX2Ac7DlyOY8-lXBW-IKcSnW5OT8DNjN1R9zAhNwofZD)
---
| Peter, I released my annual financial disclosure form, and it only took me 5 minutes – because I don’t own any stocks.

And that’s exactly what Republicans love attacking me for: not being rich.

They can attack me all they want, but I have _no_ shame. Unlike them, I haven’t abused my office to get rich off stock trading.

When I ran for Congress, I drove for Uber and took on credit card debt just to get by. And after my swearing in, I got denied from apartments in D.C. because of my credit.

So it’s funny when I get called out for experiencing the struggles that working class Americans experience everyday – because shouldn’t we have more leaders who know what it’s like? I think if we did, Congress would work a lot better than it does now.

I’m proud to represent us, and I’m committed to fighting for all of us. But it wasn’t easy for someone like me to get to Congress, so I need your support to stay here. [**_If you believe in having representatives who can actually represent the lives real people live, will you chip in $27 to my campaign before midnight? clean_body: | | Peter, I released my annual financial disclosure form, and it only took me 5 minutes – because I don’t own any stocks. And that’s exactly what Republicans love attacking me for: not being rich. They can attack me all they want, but I have no shame. Unlike them, I haven’t abused my office to get rich off stock trading. When I ran for Congress, I drove for Uber and took on credit card debt just to get by. And after my swearing in, I got denied from apartments in D.C. because of my credit. So it’s funny when I get called out for experiencing the struggles that working class Americans experience everyday – because shouldn’t we have more leaders who know what it’s like? I think if we did, Congress would work a lot better than it does now. I’m proud to represent us, and I’m committed to fighting for all of us. But it wasn’t easy for someone like me to get to Congress, so I need your support to stay here. Texas is under assault, Fellow American.

From open-border politicians, radical leftists, and faceless foreign corporations that threaten our sovereignty, safety, and our way of life, **it's time to draw a line in the sand.**

That's why I am running for Attorney General - to protect our Texas way of life.

For so long, Texas was a conservative beacon. We have a proud tradition of rising to defend our homes, our freedoms, and our communities. Texas is anchored by liberty, freedom, and self-determination.

But radical Leftists moving here are threatening to turn Texas blue. Politicians like Colin Allred are flooding our state with Hollywood and Wall Street donors.

_**They will do whatever they can to turn Texas into California or New York.**_

[**DEFEND TEXAS ★**](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a70ecbbdde33495faa7349640618a11e&_e=WJ40ThIP8NSi8CYKpP6ezTLX_T6Vt3I-7k6bnxUephlz10GPlCNwpqsByxd8uq5GkfCnE_8Wb5f5m5wZPKxGgCXCtiGcxpZ84dFxrLoAGxEj3NejldAucdt0T_-npedajzCu0bhum0eFoBjVSKWChJrepnbcd_mhPQUXnJXsFj-v2ZCggrlcO58r1N0mMnZlXOBp6-DqNrdnCakMYxK4vO6j-dmOoKKefgklphBJDWQucAkqY6b0AIhG2bAyf_JefF9IncWo1IubClINkIObNqix-y6AFSqanCuW70AQVIET8dYt3NbvrNxASMmYgaun8gxyJrvYbRMu6f0yxdAfUuO-3Kz5gkxIdAKJGW3FKBpQNy_UEuNQgYTUNBNuGbgz71Dc16DesPheGlYBoG73Tjh9-pH6QU2nSQCSoE-O1jDMwX_fNgFeiSuQnhvOWs8l9ESSesjUIN7km192rvpCvRLjCp8yDvRlkPlYoLXla-_5Q5IfGenA2qt0S5GbKYfrCv-4IxQoSs_ioFLS5YIIT9K0VZ2_wvW69Mprr9xMLspV11NY4Gk4oTGPupEY-7_RS0g5zqkrNqzW7JeAGOzpYCB_kjZNnGN5Sz7fjCQPCnFHpAlxcmtVhGnNo8AI104mjh8R8h7N-cFLsWJkWTaWd19CkuWJs-DaQhL7Y7qvAsw%3D)
I will fight every single day for our God-given rights, for our families, and for the future of Texas.

> _No more Soros-funded judges and district attorneys putting criminals on our streets.
> No more judge-made mandates that Texans pay for illegals in our public schools.
> No more communities built on Sharia law._
**[Fellow American, I need your help to carry on our Texan legacy.](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a70ecbbdde33495faa7349640618a11e&_e=WJ40ThIP8NSi8CYKpP6ezTLX_T6Vt3I-7k6bnxUephlz10GPlCNwpqsByxd8uq5GkfCnE_8Wb5f5m5wZPKxGgCXCtiGcxpZ84dFxrLoAGxEj3NejldAucdt0T_-npedajzCu0bhum0eFoBjVSKWChJrepnbcd_mhPQUXnJXsFj-v2ZCggrlcO58r1N0mMnZlXOBp6-DqNrdnCakMYxK4vO6j-dmOoKKefgklphBJDWS_S1hINBo2jikQzynQq4rmB1fXznPFHYhB4krsWzcMr68AbyQQjd52Os023Z7ZpppIrapcKkVEkT222vbqVfeJSP5QNd2SkFvUycD4UImd-68tQgL4QHulAYq5NgQNuVEIUIhmm2WH7ynMIGw6gSd9bF-BB6Pk0FOaVBcs0kkTxbsuBnq8iA2zr31E9QE3bCmOrMjOGoFgUaInOTRVpQnfZ8g_aZKzYXBbK2Su18ZMrLTvE3dj49ULRT9wLXYcLDAXIc6vr8SI3RyuqN2HDstEprqkGiaDyCXT_NQ4DY3RwLQEd85fYAUENXIGcSTU98Y-sTp51I24JaQIhZCe_865tGJuH6vaHYxrOOOJtYovc36gD8nZYKH-QT6Vp1w12y0yhCj09hUCJdZSAVX1YCwL-zcCx4BRbEA76nN7KXb1JjBmxs3aiGHm6VS77HWfV-M%3D)**

[**JOIN CHIP ROY FOR AG →**](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a70ecbbdde33495faa7349640618a11e&_e=WJ40ThIP8NSi8CYKpP6ezTLX_T6Vt3I-7k6bnxUephlz10GPlCNwpqsByxd8uq5GkfCnE_8Wb5f5m5wZPKxGgCXCtiGcxpZ84dFxrLoAGxEj3NejldAucdt0T_-npedajzCu0bhum0eFoBjVSKWChJrepnbcd_mhPQUXnJXsFj-v2ZCggrlcO58r1N0mMnZlXOBp6-DqNrdnCakMYxK4vO6j-dmOoKKefgklphBJDWTAAdZe2VIwvyxvHYMDZtmssIOucTybqw15K9zSxYCqZJS5k4orXJpNg72zcoxuHP-spYFvIE2mPorSx6F7_JsDDGumBDTXXg8B77-OB8SL0Hnr8Q3Xrxqs_0YX4zeP6mz0Gqe8pW6tc3MMTpl-lXI3zNQMbR9wBzSsUSDwo_HnZJNaAGC3nJNprhKTGf0AqDozPQiXmFG5_hP8EiCpDFIoJrmzFZ6Y8ywGzjsSbFnz4AxQIqLdh7gNgRStgA7ZRwW0jzwUWUgOQzu4rSPzEYp8m6EA7W45QQtMdDDE8SGycazz_knXNL-4VEyvQFuAWitKvueRsp83lCB6GeJaBNkx6SeSrQyQBa5_raATizd98Y8_y_S5pSuDQQx-PWjmp1Mtgidwz0MmYb_2xLEpyvndVaHHAPZJITjOJSNBtJKxiRnL3Fvss43X4bvreQJFLvk%3D)
Texas deserves an Attorney General who is unafraid to fight, unafraid to win, and unafraid to defend Texas.

It was my honor to represent Texas and the 21st Congressional District in Washington and defend President Trump's America First agenda, but it's time for a new fight.

I'll bring the same fighting spirit I brought to the DC Swamp and Deep State in Austin, but I cannot do it without the support of patriots like you.

**[Even if it's just a couple of bucks – _even $5_ – your support today will help us defend Texas.](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=a70ecbbdde33495faa7349640618a11e&_e=WJ40ThIP8NSi8CYKpP6ezTLX_T6Vt3I-7k6bnxUephlz10GPlCNwpqsByxd8uq5GkfCnE_8Wb5f5m5wZPKxGgCXCtiGcxpZ84dFxrLoAGxEj3NejldAucdt0T_-npedajzCu0bhum0eFoBjVSKWChJrepnbcd_mhPQUXnJXsFj-v2ZCggrlcO58r1N0mMnZlXOBp6-DqNrdnCakMYxK4vO6j-dmOoKKefgklphBJDWSe6Xq6Lmz5BnH84JR_XYOJbrKBdpI-r8Pn3aTjzz3QUqfunyuleVqDTEHwEFlsWpYtdrTKX3pAcR2fUia1mrUIOQF8ZUQFUvAlzpGoO26YjQwctHyOh-ZJQwv4LmZrhgohA8r-OJlfRJnCnRxQzJMhXjRSFIhuJ9sw_5_02HJEqCei042sPvYat2K8zXytFIAg2XdCul7WBeer3ePMBUnSBVYdeCYZf23LiG93MFTc3ykxOK_GRZ4ZqX1qyS8IdehTzlU8OY4N7ltAE4IGiVNpbm1aga2n0i32nB9A_Sh5ohX_nCRObSINsrcCDhaqMdIUC3BS7iQ2aSx9buOb3OOUhVSmQeQbYrVDmzSwpS_DT1NbagmQfxAJKkiKcQZBJ4foCHTFGFajHylGS0FPLAkN75K6WICOgHlYX0CQrEUOdPzdJ9rDisoYowCvPE7lr4U%3D)**

clean_body: I will fight every single day for our God-given rights | Texas is under assault, Fellow American. From open-border politicians, radical leftists, and faceless foreign corporations that threaten our sovereignty, safety, and our way of life, it's time to draw a line in the sand. That's why I am running for Attorney General - to protect our Texas way of life. For so long, Texas was a conservative beacon. We have a proud tradition of rising to defend our homes, our freedoms, and our communities. Texas is anchored by liberty, freedom, and self-determination. But radical Leftists moving here are threatening to turn Texas blue. Politicians like Colin Allred are flooding our state with Hollywood and Wall Street donors. They will do whatever they can to turn Texas into California or New York. I will fight every single day for our God-given rights, for our families, and for the future of Texas. No more Soros-funded judges and district attorneys putting criminals on our streets. No more judge-made mandates that Texans pay for illegals in our public schools. No more communities built on Sharia law. Texas deserves an Attorney General who is unafraid to fight, unafraid to win, and unafraid to defend Texas. It was my honor to represent Texas and the 21st Congressional District in Washington and defend President Trump's America First agenda, but it's time for a new fight. I'll bring the same fighting spirit I brought to the DC Swamp and Deep State in Austin, but I cannot do it without the support of patriots like you. I am counting on you, Fellow American. Let's win. Thank you, Chip Roy | | PAID FOR BY TEXANS FOR CHIP ROY Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but .

name: Yassamin Ansari
subject: The House just voted to release the Epstein files
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Yassamin Ansari for Congress
body:
|
---
Peter — this administration has done everything possible to kill our investigation into the abuses inflicted by Jeffrey Epstein and his associates, but today the House stood on the right side of history and I was proud to vote in favor of releasing the files. This is a huge moment for survivors, but it’s only the first step to delivering accountability for everyone who enabled Epstein’s abuse of minors. **This moment did not come easily.** Speaker Mike Johnson stalled for months to swear in Rep. Adelita Grijalva, who became the final signature we needed on our discharge petition to vote on the files. Donald Trump has spent a significant amount of time and energy convincing his followers that this is a “hoax.” Survivors even came and testified with groups of Members like myself to provide their firsthand accounts of the abuse they experienced. Donald Trump is already under subpoena to release the full and unredacted files RIGHT NOW. This isn’t a partisan fight, Peter, it’s about holding abusers accountable for their crimes, regardless of who they are and how much power they hold. I don’t care whose name is in there – this must come to light. [**This is the first step in a long journey to accountability. If you’re standing with us, pitch in right now.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119533789/583380799/470246576?refcode=AU_YA_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251118_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NzkxMzg2Ny1kNGM0LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qFDK60yPzHvmvqV2d1Ifabpr_JEUeUC_0SvRnM-cfDI=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119533790/583380800/220106011?express=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_YA_FR_GEN_RES_AC_20251118_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NzkxMzg2Ny1kNGM0LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qFDK60yPzHvmvqV2d1Ifabpr_JEUeUC_0SvRnM-cfDI=)

name: Blue Dog Democrats
subject: We can take back the House
body:

body:
From McCain's legacy to MAGA extremism: How Arizona's Republican Party lost its way. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119533852/583381036/-1397648958?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251118_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiY2Q2NDVjODQtZDRjNC1mMDExLTgxOTYtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=g8_LL40FMH752nOG1xRu299P2KsNK40qQ7UjXh_MzzA=)
---
Friends, **Seven years after John McCain's death, Arizona Republicans are still fighting over his legacy.** Last week, Meghan McCain responded on social media after a Turning Point Action official disparaged her father. Her message to MAGA Republicans? Keep trashing her late father, and watch how Arizona voters respond. She's not wrong. **Just look at what happened to Kari Lake.** Remember Lake's infamous moment? She asked if there were any "McCain Republicans" in the crowd at her campaign event. "Get the hell out," Lake told them. After winning her 2022 primary, she bragged: "We drove a stake through the heart of the McCain machine." And then Lake lost. _Twice._ The Arizona Republican Party's transformation is complete. Just look at the recent evidence: Andy Biggs called Democrats "domestic terrorists" and lied about healthcare on TV. Eli Crane is trying to impeach federal judges for ruling against Trump. Juan Ciscomani visited food banks after voting to slash 2 million pounds of food aid to Arizona. And they all backed Trump's $300 million vanity ballroom while _tens of millions of Americans_ struggled during the government shutdown. [**Will you support AZ Dems as we hold these extremists accountable and flip Arizona blue for good?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119533854/583381038/541746778?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251118_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiY2Q2NDVjODQtZDRjNC1mMDExLTgxOTYtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=g8_LL40FMH752nOG1xRu299P2KsNK40qQ7UjXh_MzzA=) | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119533856/583381040/79744751?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251118_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiY2Q2NDVjODQtZDRjNC1mMDExLTgxOTYtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=g8_LL40FMH752nOG1xRu299P2KsNK40qQ7UjXh_MzzA=)

Well, this wasn’t on our 2025 bingo card, Peter:
After the shutdown revealed they have NO plan, **Republicans worried about next year’s elections have suddenly realized they need to address America’s health care crisis.**
While they figure that out...here’s what Democrats have done on health care:
✅ Brought America's uninsured rate to a historic low through the ACA
✅ Created enhanced subsidies that helped 24.3 million Americans afford coverage
✅ Reduced medical debt through Medicaid expansion
✅ Fighting _right now_ to extend subsidies and keep premiums affordable
And the Republicans who are under pressure and now scrambling to put together proposals:
❌ Let enhanced subsidies expire, doubling or tripling premiums for millions of families
❌ Have no Obamacare alternative despite Trump promising one for over a year
❌ Blocked every Democratic effort to extend affordable coverage during the shutdown
❌ Want to replace Obamacare subsidies with Health Savings Accounts that only benefit wealthy Americans
**So, we’ll ask again, Peter: Who do YOU trust more to make health care affordable?**

name: Eric Swalwell
subject: About that Kash Patel hearing…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Swalwell For Congress
body:
Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District.
https://ericswalwell.com [https://ericswalwell.com]
Hi friend,

Back in September, you might recall I had a notable exchange [https://x.com/atrupar/status/1968343661969600920] with one Kash Patel. It went a little something like this:

Me: You said you don't know the number of times Trump's name appears in the [Epstein] files, so it could at least be a thousand times, is that right?

Kash Patel: You can characterize the numbers however you want.

Me: It sounds like if you don't know the number, it could at least be a thousand times.

Kash Patel: It's not.

Me: Is it at least 500 times?

Kash Patel: No.

Me: Is at least 100 times... Then what's the number?

Kash Patel: I don't know the number, but it's not that.

Me: Do you think it might be your job to know the number?

I obviously did not get a straight answer from Mr. Patel that day, but as we now know from news reports, the President’s name appears in the Epstein files multiple times.

There is still so much left to uncover, but what we have already found out is damning. I can’t believe I’ve had to pose the question of: If Trump had forced sex with an underage girl, should he still be our President?

We cannot stand for this corruption. Patel’s pathetic attempts to hide behind empty legal excuses won’t work. He can try to bury the truth to protect powerful friends, but we know the truth.

Can you chip in $25 today to help keep up the fight for accountability and justice? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-Nov2025-FR-KashPatel?&refcode=E16259-20251118-KashPatel-SL-Other&amount=25]

STAND UP TO CORRUPTION » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-Nov2025-FR-KashPatel?&refcode=E16259-20251118-KashPatel-SB-Other&amount=25]

Thanks for your support,

- Eric
DONATE TO ERIC » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-aug2025-fr-august?refcode=E16259-20251118-KashPatel-footer-Other&amount=25]
http://go.txting.io/e8bRZP [http://go.txting.io/e8bRZP]

name: =?iso-8859-13?q?Ken=FFTravius?= Coleman, United for Democracy
subject: The time to act is now
disclaimer_text: Paid for by United for Democracy
body:
The Supreme Court heard one of the most important cases for our democracy this term. | |
---
Peter, **The biggest case the Supreme Court has heard so far this term could determine whether our democracy lives or dies.** The case is called _Louisiana v. Callais_ , and it’s a challenge to Louisiana’s congressional maps, which were pretty clearly drawn and gerrymandered to dilute Black voting power in the state. The right for the people to choose their representatives is the most fundamental value in a democracy. And those who came before us bravely fought to enshrine equal rights at the ballot box in the Voting Rights Act __ of 1965. **But this Louisiana gerrymandering case before the Supreme Court gives the MAGA justices the opportunity to rip the Voting Rights Act to shreds once and for all.** If the Court sides with Louisiana Republicans, they’ll give politicians the ability to dilute the political power of Black voters across the country. Of course, Republicans are betting that less Black and POC representation means fewer votes for Democrats. But the biggest reason they want the MAGA justices to rule against the Voting Rights Act? **Congressional Republicans would get the chance to draw themselves 19 new House seats and hold on to their ill-gotten power for decades to come.** [**The Supreme Court could announce their ruling on this case any day now , but we can’t wait around for them to rip apart the**** _Voting Rights Act_****. Tell your members of Congress to protect voting rights by passing the**** _Redistricting Reform Act_****before it’s too late.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38jvf5GaclcQ7i_R9B-DMhJxpTJE3HIHzGke8tGHrJP6XXa640Zb7hMYvW6bxzrSWNfXYnKBR4cVCJA9k2qV7453KeO3DlJ9yCaZJZ20xf81IN24oTqUiafXyBP2EdMJNizhVpRX34q7YTaHSWnarF9L3W9sY97bvgVVc-YYOdgNALDKhyMBm_EVqSBLiOKe9JhduneNh1vVCnnJqC7XgrqpJFiYBYfLA8jL8HHb3uKJB8AZ2_WcSWvxbM_nEfSx4tFIolzflhF2Ymem8WbYco9WrCNzznsSLjKsVuHqY9U8Zspk-dPkw8R2f2LQ8YVWxoBiHYsjWCnjr6wjc-nDf6IqopGO7KtyUoiaWyQCWsAa0FK8DX7Dc8Uqq6KJvpex5M/4lp/MMn_Rtv2QPaGugUttylaTA/h0/h001._Bk8NdLLtUQEz5FKn5bcB5N1B8EiY3IfizBPE_WJWh4) [SIGN & SEND](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38jvf5GaclcQ7i_R9B-DMhJxpTJE3HIHzGke8tGHrJP6XXa640Zb7hMYvW6bxzrSWNfXYnKBR4cVCJA9k2qV7453KeO3DlJ9yCaZJZ20xf81IN24oTqUiafXyBP2EdMJNizhVpRX34q7YTaHSWnarF9L3W9sY97bvgVVc-YYOdgNALDKhyMBm_EVqSBLiOKe9IT_bleenLyDwIA8nKCHUrbK_r7MLx3gYpo36FIacTkE9jvPdpxxF-kXSSLm19BHl_V-PZnUVeY7TOZ_ukeCHCenyVWlajK9WHla_E6WK7Un39o_VTwAQSIbNP1bd9v7BKJOM7FfZi34CDPrsB9UIfmQ6y3Qdm17Ton3elfvAD12nP7pa0GlPNIN-9BfsQ-_ik/4lp/MMn_Rtv2QPaGugUttylaTA/h1/h001.-UjteZ3bm_F_FVjgRJfQd2fRNRQ7FdvAZI7UMzNBNzI) It’s not just Louisiana that’s fighting over redrawn Congressional maps right now. The redistricting fight is a free-for-all across the country. **In response to red state gerrymandering, blue states have started redrawing their maps to solidify Democratic voting power.** California, for example, just passed Prop 50 to fight back against the five new Congressional seats Texas Republicans created for themselves. **States and governors are stepping up because they know we can’t trust the Courts to protect our democracy.** But state-by-state action can only go so far, and it threatens to leave minority voters behind in red states across the country. That’s why Congress needs to do their job and pass legislation to combat partisan gerrymandering and protect the right to vote for everyone in America. Rep. Larson and Sen. Padilla have introduced the _Redistricting Reform Act_ to ban mid-decade redistricting efforts and require states to establish independent redistricting commissions. [**Politicians are playing games with our voting rights. And the Supreme Court is likely to stand with the extremists and rip away voting rights. Congress needs to do its job now, and pass the**** _Redistricting Reform Act_****.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38jvf5GaclcQ7i_R9B-DMhJxpTJE3HIHzGke8tGHrJP6XXa640Zb7hMYvW6bxzrSWNfXYnKBR4cVCJA9k2qV7453KeO3DlJ9yCaZJZ20xf81IN24oTqUiafXyBP2EdMJNizhVpRX34q7YTaHSWnarF9L3W9sY97bvgVVc-YYOdgNALDKhyMBm_EVqSBLiOKe9Iw_awnS-7SvHXxi5SpLy3JELWeGvNR_9lv7unIprGIh5ofLIoAU7Hvaop-rWgmkfXYKbyGGQcCQiV2R1wwgvheQB-deJAC-9MHcZRLNsT5diYB0MEoiGE2N-jXa5p0tqR1zl4skyDrTrp-78cyJtovmWuMa6-hcoekMgJbv1H1_JZGC1KzgEDEkW7xyQ_7JYU/4lp/MMn_Rtv2QPaGugUttylaTA/h2/h001.pchU4vgXHUP4FAlphNUu5mqcajQ6i7LaYwbx5HGSHSo) Thank you for fighting for voting rights, Ken’Travius Coleman

clean_body: The Supreme Court heard one of the most important cases for our democracy this term. | | Peter, The biggest case the Supreme Court has heard so far this term could determine whether our democracy lives or dies. The case is called Louisiana v. Callais , and it’s a challenge to Louisiana’s congressional maps, which were pretty clearly drawn and gerrymandered to dilute Black voting power in the state. The right for the people to choose their representatives is the most fundamental value in a democracy. And those who came before us bravely fought to enshrine equal rights at the ballot box in the Voting Rights Act of 1965. But this Louisiana gerrymandering case before the Supreme Court gives the MAGA justices the opportunity to rip the Voting Rights Act to shreds once and for all. If the Court sides with Louisiana Republicans, they’ll give politicians the ability to dilute the political power of Black voters across the country. Of course, Republicans are betting that less Black and POC representation means fewer votes for Democrats. But the biggest reason they want the MAGA justices to rule against the Voting Rights Act? Congressional Republicans would get the chance to draw themselves 19 new House seats and hold on to their ill-gotten power for decades to come. It’s not just Louisiana that’s fighting over redrawn Congressional maps right now. The redistricting fight is a free-for-all across the country. In response to red state gerrymandering, blue states have started redrawing their maps to solidify Democratic voting power. California, for example, just passed Prop 50 to fight back against the five new Congressional seats Texas Republicans created for themselves. States and governors are stepping up because they know we can’t trust the Courts to protect our democracy. But state-by-state action can only go so far, and it threatens to leave minority voters behind in red states across the country. That’s why Congress needs to do their job and pass legislation to combat partisan gerrymandering and protect the right to vote for everyone in America. Rep. Larson and Sen. Padilla have introduced the Redistricting Reform Act to ban mid-decade redistricting efforts and require states to establish independent redistricting commissions. Thank you for fighting for voting rights, Ken’Travius Coleman United for Democracy Paid for by United for Democracy. | | Sent via . body:

**$5** , Peter.
That is the contribution we’re asking you to make today — to Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign here in Georgia.
**$5** because winning in 2026 is our only hope to regain Congressional power and reverse our ongoing descent into one-man rule.
**$5** because if the GOP succeeds in flipping this seat red, they will lock in control of the Senate for the long-term.
**$5** because there is no path back from the brink of authoritarianism without defending this seat — the very same one that secured the Democratic majority in 2021.
You might think a single $5 contribution can’t make a difference in a race like this. But right now, as you’re reading this, lots and lots of people like you are chipping in their own $5 — _and they’re hoping you’ll do the same because they know it adds up FAST when we’re all doing our part._

name: Kathy Barnette
subject: Black Conservatives: Sacrifices & Election Losses. Are You Ready?
body:
Imagine being Black and then saying, "I'm a conservative and I'm going to live like a conservative, and I'm gonna talk like a conservative, and I'm gonna vote like a conservative." And then losing everything.

name: Mark Warner
subject: Can I call and extend my gratitude?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119534071/583381983/1841968466?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_CallFollowUp_251118_T1_1_a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODdhNzFiMy1kNWM0LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sGgFfSPuXcuu8V-2JQRTg6XdFN2x9S6TQ45aG0AIV5g=) Derek,

I don’t take your support for granted, and I can't emphasize enough how much it means to me that you're a part of this team.

In fact, one of my favorite parts of this job is being able to connect with grassroots supporters like you, Derek – the folks that are standing with me every day to push back on Trump’s harmful, dangerous agenda.

_That’s why I’m looking forward to calling a few donors personally and expressing my gratitude._

Unfortunately, we came up short of our 1,000 mid-month donor goal, which means that we’re not on track to reach our fundraising goal for November – and _that_ means we’re in a bad position ahead of our public filing deadline next month.

[_**So, Derek, before I start making calls to grassroots donors, I’m hoping you’ll consider chipping in to my reelection campaign today to protect this seat and take back the Senate for Democrats so we can finally put a check on this Trump administration’s chaos, corruption, and incompetence. If you do, there's a chance I'll be calling you soon to say thank you.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119534072/583381984/1841968466?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_CallFollowUp_251118_T1_1_a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODdhNzFiMy1kNWM0LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sGgFfSPuXcuu8V-2JQRTg6XdFN2x9S6TQ45aG0AIV5g=) name: Christopher Street Project via Team Titone
subject: Trans Awareness Week
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brianna for Treasurer

This week is Trans Awareness Week, a time to honor our community’s resilience and remind the world that trans people are here to stay. | | | [](http://links.briannaforco.com/s/c/Z1fPllz_BYl_HmbTkIeBp3Pt_yWE_Fkv0OLJLKZXlmtYN3G_73W-rcGsSttUMu_VwKJQlMiMZ-CH8xuUHThTkJ1TUxH7y-5Z69n9xFy5_1rTxCc-MGA5IPU8b1e6geBDsuTQBdb5JZs12fn5STQuzYpi62BMdG-twXXQ7zpVJ4oK5KE_xbvkK9MDJgsAbQ5727L37AKL6kzAMPJcMaYEDD5aXCmf7_UU2dSYuTx_GfDg_UC44bE59rHkadnltdwfwWwPkY89o3vcCbdyGX-4phILbsoiod7ChAqml9XYFUlG8v_hmW4RZUkSsWjOenbAB96p6Lp3n-MxCZrFUwp7LVGkE5v9x-8VgxSjF42nc4c3eqfAGKZt-oMhEAmA39peBJl1ivVEiynzAyuJPG8CasRdeOem8aoI2h3eQX4ywUbudriPZsSdvLfdZGLNC95D_GGS-DvgsBjJmQjX1w57j5qzwbcGaYN7LE31wDBwQ4HoIi3B5yLczIYe4FNJ/qiclLwTZViawvcEL_m8Vzfh8ORQgAhUo/15) Peter, This week is **Trans Awareness Week** , a time to honor our community’s resilience and remind the world that trans people are here to stay. From the ballot box to the halls of Congress to the streets, **Christopher Street Project is building the political power to hold policymakers accountable for trans rights and fight back against every effort to erase us.** Rooted in the legacy of trans and queer activists who came before us, our work blends culture, politics, and direct support to sustain trans and queer joy as a form of resistance. We are changing the public narrative on trans issues and disarming the obsessive fixation on our lives, because our stories are about power, not pity. As we mark **Trans Awareness Week** and approach the **Transgender Day of Remembrance** , we’re also launching our **End of Year Giving Campaign** on **December 2nd** to ensure this fight continues into 2026 and beyond. Every dollar fuels organizing, endorsements, and advocacy that make trans political power undeniable. The more they try to silence us, the louder we will get. **[Will you split a donation today between Brianna Titone and Christopher Street Project to power our work and stand with us during Trans Awareness Week?](http://links.briannaforco.com/s/c/D4kZxWp_JETxNejQz9xnzaujDqK8jvHTnlIaNJ3VEqxOOUUNSMG4nW8TELW6JVJiCK-5tNSvaOqyy2y4tEHMAl39JwuRQvp6jzz4pvCZCDtWddGxx_a5NNURWSwDiczNkegrOTv2l0QAr5TC3NufcmpUQ9WeY5cFOacvtC9ZppVXQfxohbaesivcuOfxl8EitympTHl4I5FFbs3_nUM0Aq8y8iaC7Uz2d4yltFzvLm_Em4g0ANozSy2Xv342nEO974LSkvPGM-uLohNgItfolqmuuXw-c9mj05hIA9A2KmzRTEpr_b5sn3HqNAiE0udwVVzEX08ujYon-kATcadTMTvfewXLg7qcc-GEDUSqwtimTnP6JRTi7sf7AgB2t3rWaGFwlT8piM1qA8sGmU_GXkfja0e2JkQBa3xYz86cAkiEByRY_brwg1Q9pMwCO_YAOmLLpa_J_oPJLn-VTQx9nGvdlwKl9vrTWl58W18e6TlJ6YJqc7eGfSvLvBSX/XbWI9XxE-lgcWE-qIHLgqmpWxuLEFFcv/15)** Thank you, Christopher Street Project

name: Chris Ahuja
subject: Accountability Starts Here
body:
Women are constituents. Not entertainment.

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=06e59ef9c3&e=5a6ee82cdb)
| | | | | | Dear Peter,**Brad Sherman’s antics quickly went viral this weekend,** and have now gone worldwide, with reporters in England, India, and elsewhere writing about the conduct unbecoming of a sitting U.S. Congressman.**Not a good look for the 32nd Congressional District and the United States.**
Our representatives should embody integrity and accountability. Yet Congressman Brad Sherman has repeatedly shown judgment that undermines both.
From excusing inappropriate conduct on a public flight caused by wild “algorithms” to asking if American foreign policy would be “harmed by releasing the Epstein files,” Sherman reveals a troubling pattern. He treats accountability as optional. **That’s unacceptable for someone entrusted with the responsibility of representation in Washington.**
Beyond Brad Sherman’s repeated missteps, we cannot allow leaders who embarrass us on the national and global stage to keep holding power, and stay in office just because they have been there for the past three decades. **We must demand representatives who fight for justice, transparency, and moral clarity – and who don’t ogle at scantily clad women on IPads on public flights.**
If you agree that our elected officials, our representatives in Washington, should not be a national and global embarrassment – that our members of congress should not repeatedly present a pattern of judgment and transparency ineptitude, then [**I need you in my corner.**](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=b1fe4bbdc6&e=5a6ee82cdb)
**I’m running for Congress to build a movement rooted in courage and accountability**. Your support powers grassroots organizing, amplifies progressive voices, and ensures that Washington reflects our values. Enough of status quo failures.
[**Join me, and join our movement to make things right.**](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=d409e65761&e=5a6ee82cdb)

---
| [Donate](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=9a8651c279&e=5a6ee82cdb)
---
| |

**No Fear. No Silence.
**[****](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=1a7828d1bb&e=5a6ee82cdb)**Chris Ahuja****Democrat for Congress, CA-32**
---
| | | | | | | | | [ View email in browser](https://mailchi.mp/chrisahuja/ahuja-eom103125-11043443?e=5a6ee82cdb)
Christopher Ahuja for Congress * 21255 Burbank Blvd Ste 120 * Woodland Hills, CA 91367-6669 * USA
[update your preferences](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/profile?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=45384ee532&e=5a6ee82cdb&c=11214b0e16) or [unsubscribe](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=45384ee532&t=b&e=5a6ee82cdb&c=11214b0e16)

name: Health Care Poll
subject: Do you approve of Trump's handling of health care?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | **ALERT: Nearly TWO-THIRDS of Americans _disapprove_ of Trump's health care agenda**
[](https://go.dscc.org/go/22268?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20251118-HealthCarePoll-TrumpHealthCare1QS-ETG-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20251118-HealthCarePoll-TrumpHealthCare1QS-ETG-sl1&akid=21791%2E9716520%2ExTsQva)
**We need to hear from you:****Do you approve of Trump's handling of health care?**
| | | [Yes](https://go.dscc.org/go/22268?ask=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20251118-HealthCarePoll-TrumpHealthCare1QS-ETG-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20251118-HealthCarePoll-TrumpHealthCare1QS-ETG-sl1&akid=21791%2E9716520%2ExTsQva)

It's not hard to see why, team:

He's ripping health insurance away from MILLIONS of Americans. He's elevating anti-science conspiracy theorists to lead our nation's public health. He's even instructing Republicans to _refuse_ to take action to prevent health care costs from skyrocketing. **Trump's handling of this life-or-death issue has been absolutely awful!**

Americans are rejecting Trump's health care agenda _in droves_ , and we need to make sure you are too. We're looking for 10,000 responses by MIDNIGHT -- so please, **be one of the first Democrats in Pennsylvania to submit your response for 11/18:**

**Do you approve of Trump's handling of health care?**

name: Katie Porter
subject: California’s housing crisis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
I’m running for governor to bring new leadership to address the housing crisis.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40438%2E3422442%2EAJTXe5)
Peter,
California’s housing crisis is something families feel every single day. For renters, homeowners, and retirees, our system is too expensive and too slow.
I’m running for governor to bring new leadership to address the housing crisis. **[If you’re with me, can you chip in $5 to our campaign today?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251118_1_ak.nj2gkw&refcode=em20251118-40438&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40438%2E3422442%2EAJTXe5)**
As governor, I’ll champion policies to speed up construction and remove the barriers that raise costs and delay the homes we desperately need. We have to greenlight innovative building strategies that prioritize housing for all sorts of people who make California thrive: our workforce, students, seniors, and families with young children.
Homelessness prevention is part of the solution, too. By investing in interim housing and data-driven prevention, we will dramatically reduce street homelessness _before_ it happens.
Our next governor needs to be ready to overcome the special interests and entrenched opposition that have made housing unaffordable. I’ve already gone toe-to-toe with some of the toughest opponents in Washington—and I’ll bring that same grit to tackling California’s housing crisis.
**[Leadership means doing the right thing for our state and our families—and as governor, I won’t hesitate to build more housing and bring down costs. But to do that and win this race, I need your help. Can you donate $5 today?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251118_1_ak.nj2gkw&refcode=em20251118-40438&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=40438%2E3422442%2EAJTXe5)**

clean_body: I’m running for governor to bring new leadership to address the housing crisis. Peter, California’s housing crisis is something families feel every single day. For renters, homeowners, and retirees, our system is too expensive and too slow. I’m running for governor to bring new leadership to address the housing crisis. As governor, I’ll champion policies to speed up construction and remove the barriers that raise costs and delay the homes we desperately need. We have to greenlight innovative building strategies that prioritize housing for all sorts of people who make California thrive: our workforce, students, seniors, and families with young children. Homelessness prevention is part of the solution, too. By investing in interim housing and data-driven prevention, we will dramatically reduce street homelessness before it happens. Our next governor needs to be ready to overcome the special interests and entrenched opposition that have made housing unaffordable. I’ve already gone toe-to-toe with some of the toughest opponents in Washington—and I’ll bring that same grit to tackling California’s housing crisis. Thank you, Katie Porter Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change.

name: Team Kern
subject: We Must Protect ICE
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Vote Kern]( "Paid For By Vote Kern")
body:
The left wants chaos on the streets |
---
| | [ ](https://votekern.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2add33eab54cf78e2fdebc3c8&id=d46900a0fc&e=b78dc6f299)
---
|
---
| **Anthony Kern For State Senate:

We must protect ICE and keep criminals off the streets**
---
|
---
| Supporters and Friends,

_**The Democratic Party has become a bastion of lawlessness**_ , undermining not only the rule of law, but the patriotic men and women who work tirelessly to uphold it. Recently, a Democratic state senator here in Arizona shared on social media a warning to illegal aliens that ICE agents were in the area and that they should flee lawful apprehension. _**These are people who have broken our laws**_ , disrespected our country, and who have no place in our communities—but this Democratic senator made it her priority to protect criminals over her own constituents. This is a prime example of why we need strong, conservative leadership in Phoenix, _and why I am running to provide that for LD-27 in the state senate._ Having worked in law enforcement myself, **I was a friend to all peace officers during my eight years in the legislature**. And if given the honor of serving you again, I will be a resolute ally to President Trump and his no-nonsense border policies, **including giving unwavering support to the patriotic men and women in ICE.** I understand that when it comes to having a functional government and sovereign borders, everything else falls by the wayside: _securing America and Arizona comes first_. So when the Democratic Party begins to tear at our laws and the very fabric of our country, I will be standing there ready to do battle, arm in arm with dutiful ICE agents and in service of LD-27. If you believe in getting these criminal aliens off our streets—and helping the agents doing their jobs—[**then I need you to contribute $25 to my campaign.**](https://votekern.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2add33eab54cf78e2fdebc3c8&id=7a2f4493da&e=b78dc6f299) The Trump administration has already got the ball rolling. We can’t falter because of ineffective representation. **[Chip in $25 to my campaign, and LD-27 will receive the dedicated, conservative champion it needs and deserves.](https://votekern.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2add33eab54cf78e2fdebc3c8&id=216ec670d7&e=b78dc6f299)**

Peter ,
Tennessee’s “safe red” special election just got real.
Cook Political Report just shifted TN-07 from “Solid Republican” to Leans Republican, and the GOP is panicking. Republican Matt Van Epps is suddenly on air attacking Democrat Aftyn Behn for opposing ICE raids – a sign his internal numbers are tightening fast.
Here’s their real problem:
A special election wedged between Thanksgiving means turnout will decide everything. Democrats are energized. Republicans aren’t.
That’s why the RNC is dropping six figures. That’s why MAGA groups are scrambling. And that’s why we have to scale up now.
Will you rush $10 or anything you can to GOTV PAC so we can expand field operations and drive early voting before Dec. 2? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email11182025]

Get Out the Vote PAC mobilizes voters often overlooked by mainstream campaigns to help Democratic candidates win in the most competitive districts.
Our professional phone bankers specialize in long- and short-term GOTV campaigns, event registration, voting assistance in heavily gerrymandered states, fundraising, and call time management. Our mission is to flip red districts blue, any elected position from dog walker to Congress member.
Learn more at: gotv.vote [https://www.gotv.vote/]

Get Out the Vote PAC is registered with the Federal Election Commission as a hybrid Political Action Committee (PAC) with FEC ID C00839407. clean_body: [] Peter , Tennessee’s “safe red” special election just got real. Cook Political Report just shifted TN-07 from “Solid Republican” to Leans Republican, and the GOP is panicking. Republican Matt Van Epps is suddenly on air attacking Democrat Aftyn Behn for opposing ICE raids – a sign his internal numbers are tightening fast. Here’s their real problem: A special election wedged between Thanksgiving means turnout will decide everything. Democrats are energized. Republicans aren’t. That’s why the RNC is dropping six figures. That’s why MAGA groups are scrambling. And that’s why we have to scale up now. Will you rush $10 or anything you can to GOTV PAC so we can expand field operations and drive early voting before Dec. 2? [] Donate Now [] - GOTV PAC [] Get Out the Vote PAC mobilizes voters often overlooked by mainstream campaigns to help Democratic candidates win in the most competitive districts. Our professional phone bankers specialize in long- and short-term GOTV campaigns, event registration, voting assistance in heavily gerrymandered states, fundraising, and call time management. Our mission is to flip red districts blue, any elected position from dog walker to Congress member. Husband. Brother. Son. Northern Nash High Varsity Basketball alum (and a graduate of NC public schools from 1st grade through law school). A former Governor, Attorney General, and legislator before that — with a winning record in the toughest campaigns. Your Democratic candidate for the U.S. Senate from North Carolina. **What I stand for:** Affordable health care and housing. Lower prices. Good jobs. Strong schools. A liveable planet. Abortion access. Voting rights. Feeding kids who are hungry. Doing right by this country even when powerful politicians would rather I stay silent. **Why I need you:** My race for the U.S. Senate is one of the most competitive in the country, and Republicans see me as their top threat. There’s a long road ahead of us, and if we’re going to win, we need the resources to take on everything the GOP can throw at us. [I know how to win NC. I know I can’t do it alone. subject: Release all the damn files. Now.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Today I spoke on the House floor ahead of the vote to release the Epstein Files.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20251118-5637-footer&t=1&akid=5637%2E420207%2ELzbdMA)
Peter,
Today I spoke on the House floor ahead of the vote to compel the Justice Department to release the Epstein Files, and my message was simple:
**Release all the damn files. Now.**
Thousands of girls were abused by Jeffrey Epstein and his massive and powerful ring of pedophiles and protectors of pedophiles. They carried a burden that they never should have had to bear.
The least we can do is answer their call to justice by voting to release the files — and that’s exactly what we did today, despite all the cover-up attempts by the Trump administration _and_ the Speaker of the House.
With this vote, we’ve made it clear that there is no protection for pedophiles, and no protection for the rich and powerful men of _either_ party who participated or turned a blind eye to the horrific abuses of young children.
It’s time to get justice for survivors and the full truth for the American people.
In solidarity,
Pramila Jayapal
